extraction.one_line_summary: TRENTIN Matteo attacked just before the final Cat 3 climb (5.2km, 58km to go) from a 33-rider breakaway, soloing to victory with 30 seconds.
tactical_patterns: Trentin attacked immediately before climb start rather than on it, gaining surprise advantage before chasers organized on gradient.
Mitchelton-Scott used multi-stage attrition strategy: Trentin previously did 200km break, conditioning field to ignore future moves.
Sagan deliberately abandoned stage 5 minutes behind peloton after learning heat-based time cut exemption applied to GC group.
Seven teams without stage wins created desperate 33-rider break on zero kilometer, diluting chase organization from start.

rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: positive: timed attack precisely before Cat 3 climb after failed earlier efforts, showing tactical patience unusual for breakaway racing
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: gaming time cut rules by finishing 5min behind after hearing elimination based on contender group, prioritizing tomorrow
rider_signals.KING Ben: negative: dropped from decisive Trentin move after executing two hard attacks that emptied him before critical climb
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: negative: visible in chase behind Trentin but unable to close 30-second gap on Cat 3, questioning climbing form
